original.scope_note: D5C fixed-PFA RA smoke on validation/test split; no model training

best_physical.scope_note: D5C fixed-PFA RA smoke on validation/test split; no model training

best_empirical.scope_note: D5C fixed-PFA RA smoke on validation/test split; no model training

decision_from_config.verdict: RA prior result is sensitive but not physically restored
decision_from_config.next_step: Keep RA inconclusive; optional RCA follow-up should justify angle-axis physics before any RA rerun.

findings.observation: Original D5C RA weak fixed-PFA hit rate was reproduced exactly: 0.030534.
findings.interpretation: The 0.0305 number is not a dead-code artifact; it follows from the implemented fixed-PFA RA path.
findings.implication: Do not dismiss the prior D5C RA result as a logging bug.
findings.next_step: Keep the original D5C RA row as the aligned baseline for any rerun.

findings.observation: D1A+ local RA projection hit rate was 0.876297; on the D5C weak test subset, D1A-style hit rate was 0.683206, while D5C fixed-PFA weak hit rate was 0.030534.
findings.interpretation: Most of the 0.8763 vs 0.0305 gap is criterion/scope: local contrast sanity vs weak-only fixed-PFA thresholding.
findings.implication: The old D1A+ number cannot be used to claim D5C RA fixed-PFA success.
findings.next_step: Report both criteria side by side in any follow-up.

findings.observation: `atan2(px, py)` is the only formula matching the manifest FOV and has lower mean angle error (34.68 deg) than empirical alternatives that increase weak hits.
findings.interpretation: No physically justified sign, coordinate, or degrees/radians bug is established.
findings.implication: Do not switch to `atan2(py, px)`, sign flip, radians misuse, or unshifted bins just because a sweep row is higher.
findings.next_step: If RA is revisited, calibrate the angle axis physically before changing mapping.

findings.observation: Best physically plausible mask row improves weak hit rate only to 0.094118; best empirical row reaches 0.184314 but has mean angle error 63.17 deg.
findings.interpretation: RA is mask-sensitive, but the improvement is not a clean restoration of projection quality.
findings.implication: RA remains inconclusive; a wider mask is not enough to claim a bug fix.
findings.next_step: Prioritize RD; keep RA only as optional calibrated smoke follow-up.

findings.observation: Raw ADC shape is 128x255x4x2; angle FFT uses axis 1 after RX/TX concatenation into 128x255x8.
findings.interpretation: No sample/chirp axis mix-up was found, but the RA map is still a rough virtual-array smoke map.
findings.implication: RA evidence should be qualified as smoke-only because no TDM-MIMO phase compensation/calibration is shown.
findings.next_step: Do not use RA for method claims without a separate calibrated RA validation.

overall_interpretation: RA low hit rate is mainly a fixed-PFA/scope/mask-sensitivity issue, not a proven angle-coordinate bug.
recommended_route: Keep RA inconclusive and prioritize RD-only supplementary work; do not enter D6.
